gaps: No existing test case covers the DFU (Device Firmware Update) flow on the Software Update page.
No existing test case covers headset disconnection during an active update process.
No existing test case verifies error handling when a headset disconnects mid-DFU (e.g., showing an error message vs. navigating to an incorrect page like 'Three Easy Steps').
All existing test cases focus on onboarding, EULA, splash screen, and location permissions — none relate to DFU, Software Update page, or device connectivity during firmware updates.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-001: Verify DFU flow starts correctly on Software Update page
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. Observe the progress indicator or status message on the Software Update page.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU process starts successfully. The Software Update page displays a progress bar or status message indicating the update is in progress. The user remains on the Software Update page.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-002: Verify error message is displayed when headset disconnects mid-DFU
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. While the DFU is in progress, disconnect the headset (e.g., turn off Bluetooth, move out of range, or manually disconnect via system settings).
6. Observe the screen behavior and any messages displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: An error message is displayed on the Software Update page indicating that the headset has disconnected and the update has failed or paused. The user is NOT navigated away from the Software Update page to the 'Three Easy Steps' page or any other onboarding screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-003: Verify user is NOT redirected to Three Easy Steps page on headset disconnection during DFU
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. While the DFU is in progress, disconnect the headset (e.g., turn off Bluetooth, move out of range, or manually disconnect via system settings).
6. Check the current screen/page displayed in the application.
generated_testcases.expected: The application remains on the Software Update page. The 'Three Easy Steps' page is NOT displayed. The user sees an appropriate error or reconnection prompt on the Software Update page.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-004: Verify error message content and retry option on headset disconnection during DFU
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. While the DFU is in progress, disconnect the headset (e.g., turn off Bluetooth, move out of range, or manually disconnect via system settings).
6. Observe the error message text and available UI elements.
7. Reconnect the headset and check if a 'Retry' or 'Resume' option is available.
8. Tap the 'Retry' or 'Resume' button if present.
generated_testcases.expected: A clear error message is displayed (e.g., 'Headset disconnected. Update failed.' or 'Connection lost. Please reconnect your headset.'). A 'Retry' or 'Resume' button is available after reconnection. Tapping it restarts or resumes the DFU process on the Software Update page.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-005: Verify DFU error handling when headset disconnects at different stages of the update
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. Disconnect the headset at the beginning of the DFU process (0-10% progress).
6. Observe the error handling behavior.
7. Reconnect the headset and restart the DFU process.
8. Disconnect the headset in the middle of the DFU process (40-60% progress).
9. Observe the error handling behavior.
10. Reconnect the headset and restart the DFU process.
11. Disconnect the headset near the end of the DFU process (80-99% progress).
12. Observe the error handling behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: At all stages of the DFU process (beginning, middle, near end), the application displays an appropriate error message on the Software Update page. The user is never redirected to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. The error handling is consistent regardless of when the disconnection occurs.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-006: Verify DFU flow with headset reconnection after disconnection error
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. While the DFU is in progress, disconnect the headset.
6. Verify the error message is displayed on the Software Update page.
7. Reconnect the headset (e.g., turn Bluetooth back on, bring headset back in range).
8. Wait for the application to detect the reconnection.
9. Observe the UI state on the Software Update page.
generated_testcases.expected: After reconnection, the application detects the headset and updates the UI accordingly. The user sees a 'Retry' or 'Resume' option on the Software Update page. The user is NOT navigated to the 'Three Easy Steps' page or any onboarding screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-007: Verify DFU error when headset battery dies during update
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. Simulate the headset battery dying (e.g., let battery drain to 0% or simulate low battery shutdown).
6. Observe the screen behavior and any messages displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: An error message is displayed on the Software Update page indicating that the headset has disconnected due to low battery or power loss. The user is NOT navigated to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. The user remains on the Software Update page with an appropriate error message.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-008: Verify DFU error when Bluetooth is turned off during update
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Navigate to the Software Update page.
3. Ensure a paired headset is connected and has a firmware update available.
4. Tap the 'Update' or 'Start DFU' button to begin the firmware update.
5. While the DFU is in progress, turn off Bluetooth from the device's system settings.
6. Observe the screen behavior and any messages displayed.
7. Turn Bluetooth back on and wait for reconnection.
8. Observe the UI state on the Software Update page.
generated_testcases.expected: An error message is displayed on the Software Update page indicating that the connection was lost (e.g., 'Bluetooth turned off. Update failed.'). The user is NOT navigated to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. After Bluetooth is turned back on and the headset reconnects, a 'Retry' option appears on the Software Update page.
